name: Twice-Baked Ratatouille-Stuffed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1 small red bell pepper – cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cube the eggplant, toss with salt, and arrange in a single layer on a large colander. Let drain for 20 minutes. Pat dry with paper towels.
2. Bake or microwave the potatoes until soft.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
4. In a large nonstick skillet lightly sprayed with oil and a few tablespoons water, cook over medium-high heat:
a. Combine onion, garlic, and eggplant; cover and steam for 4 minutes, adding more water if necessary to prevent sticking.
b. Add pepper, zucchini, tomatoes, and vinegar. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until all vegetables are tender.
5. Slice off the top third of each potato lengthwise and scoop out the flesh, leaving a 1/2-inch shell (reserve for another use).
6. Spoon 2 tablespoons ricotta into each potato.
7. Fill potatoes with ratatouille and sprinkle with 2 tablespoons Parmesan.
8. Bake in oven at 375°F (190°C) for 35 minutes. Alternatively, place potatoes in a microwave-safe dish, cover with wax paper, and cook on high for 4 minutes, rotating once.
